No Poverty.reason: The course topic 'World Politics and the Global South' (Week 10) implicitly covers development strategies and poverty issues in developing nations, aligning with Target 1.b.
evidence: World Politics and the Global South

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the syllabus directly relates to hunger, food security, or sustainable agriculture.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the syllabus directly relates to health, well-being, or specific health challenges.

Quality Education.reason: As a university course, it inherently provides tertiary education (Target 4.3). The core competence '國際公民社會參與、關懷能力' (international civil society participation, care ability) directly aligns with education for global citizenship (Target 4.7).
evidence: 國際公民社會參與、關懷能力

Gender Equality.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the syllabus directly relates to gender equality or women's empowerment.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the syllabus directly relates to energy, renewables, or energy efficiency.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the syllabus directly relates to decent work, economic growth targets, or specific labor rights.

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the syllabus directly relates to industry, innovation, or infrastructure development.

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course explicitly covers 'World Politics and the Global South' (Week 10), which directly addresses inequalities between nations and their representation in global institutions, strongly aligning with Target 10.6.
evidence: World Politics and the Global South

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the syllabus directly relates to sustainable urbanization, housing, or community development.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the syllabus directly relates to responsible consumption, production patterns, or resource efficiency.

Climate Action.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the syllabus directly relates to climate change, mitigation, or adaptation.

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the syllabus directly relates to marine ecosystems or ocean health.

Life on Land.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the syllabus directly relates to terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, or land degradation.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course explicitly covers 'The Threat of Armed Conflict to the World' and 'Great Power Relations and Rivalries,' directly addressing peace and conflict (Target 16.1). The '模擬聯合國會議' (Model UN Conference) activity further reinforces engagement with international institutions and governance (Target 16.6, 16.8).
evidence: The Threat of Armed Conflict to the World

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course explicitly covers 'Nonstate Actors and the Quest for Global Community' and '各種成員之間如何互動' (how various members interact), which are central to understanding and fostering multi-stakeholder partnerships for sustainable development (Target 17.16).
evidence: 各種成員之間如何互動
